objective: For: designing the business model for a brand-new / zero-to-one idea from scratch.
Fires when: user has a new/early idea with NO revenue model yet and asks how to make money / monetize / structure it. Redirects to business-model-enhancer when a model already exists.
Wedge: converges on ONE recommended model (who-pays / monetization / GTM / moat) via diverge->pre-mortem->converge, where the enhancer only stress-tests an EXISTING model.

Produces: a chat recommendation — one model + why it beats the alternatives + strongest runner-up & tradeoff + riskiest assumption + cheapest validation. Tight intake first; ambition (bootstrapped vs venture) steers the optimization.
inPractice: Triggers on new/early ideas needing a business model — 'how do I make money from this?', 'what's the right revenue model for my idea?', 'turn this idea into a viable business'.
Checks the boundary first: if a revenue model already exists, redirects to business-model-enhancer instead of designing over it.
Runs a tight intake (who pays · problem · ambition · cost driver) before generating; never invents a market.
Diverges into 2-3 distinct model archetypes → pre-mortems each (lens steered by bootstrapped vs venture) → converges on ONE recommended model.
Outputs a decision: recommended model (who-pays/pricing/GTM/moat) + why it wins + runner-up & tradeoff + riskiest assumption + cheapest validation, with unverified inputs labeled.
Guards: no generic canvas dump, no invented market facts, no one-size ambition dogma.

shape.rationale: One coherent job/output; no host pipeline; kept as a sharp sibling of business-model-enhancer (cross-reference boundary, not a branch or a data handoff).

uniqueWedge: Unlike business-model-enhancer (which requires an existing model and only stress-tests additions), Business Model Architect designs the model for a brand-new idea from a blank slate — who-pays / monetization structure / GTM / moat — and converges on one recommended model with the runner-up, riskiest assumption, and cheapest validation.

annotations: [!OVERLAP] shares diverge->converge engine + business-model domain with business-model-enhancer; boundary is new-vs-existing model
[!COLLISION] gray-zone monetization prompts — arena must confirm
[!COLLISION]-RESOLVED — boundary holds in arena + enforced by Workflow step 1

decisions.decision: Output = converge to ONE recommended model (diverge->pre-mortem->commit)
decisions.rationale: User picked decision-engine over menu/canvas; keeps it out of [!GENERIC].

decisions.decision: Thin input -> tight intake first (max ~5, gaps only); never invent a market
decisions.rationale: Guards the top failure mode (hallucinated market).

decisions.decision: Ambition (bootstrapped vs venture) is a first-class intake dimension that steers the model + pre-mortem lens
decisions.rationale: Recommended option; user switched to auto before answering. Flippable at final gate.

decisions.decision: Boundary vs business-model-enhancer: redirect when a revenue model already exists
